panels.title: Client Capability / Preamble (Weakest-Link Assessment)
panels.description: Per-session hardware & network capability from videocall_client_info (the console-log preamble in metric form). The FIRST thing to read: sort by capability_score / cores to find the weakest-link participant. cores, gpu_family, architecture, network_effective_type are static per session. value is always 1; only the labels carry information.

panels.title: Client Memory: JS Heap + WASM + Agent (#1032)
panels.description: Unified per-client memory pressure (merges the former JS-heap and non-heap panels). JS heap used (Chrome only) + WASM linear memory (WebAssembly.Memory.buffer.byteLength, always available — the multi-GB decode/jitter-buffer pressure that drives freezes) + JS heap limit + total agent memory (measureUserAgentSpecificMemory, Chrome + crossOriginIsolated only, may be absent for many clients).

targets.expr: 100 * sum by (room)(rate(relay_viewport_filtered_total{room=~"$meeting"}[5m])) / clamp_min(sum by (room)(rate(relay_viewport_filtered_total{room=~"$meeting"}[5m])) + sum by (room)(rate(relay_viewport_forwarded_total{room=~"$meeting"}[5m])), 1)

panels.title: Transport Drops/sec
panels.description: Rate of transport-layer packet drops due to backpressure. WT = WebTransport (writable locked), WS = WebSocket (buffer full).

panels.title: Keyframe Requests/sec
panels.description: Rate of PLI (keyframe) requests sent. Spikes indicate video decode failures — the receiver is asking the sender to re-sync.

panels.title: Top-5 Slow Receivers (per-session drop attribution)
panels.description: Names the slow-draining receiver (finding #5, the RELAY-2 'Outbound channel full for session X' pattern). topk(5) of per-session outbound-channel drop rate from relay_session_drops_total. Map the winning session_id to a participant via the Client Capability table (id 60) or the peer map. (Populates after backend PR #TBD deploys.)

panels.title: Inbound Mailbox Overflow /sec — FLEET-WIDE (#1057)
panels.description: The room-wide-freeze signature (#1057): inbound actor-mailbox overflow drops by transport. FLEET-WIDE — relay_inbound_mailbox_drops_total carries only a 'transport' label (no room/meeting), so this is NOT filtered by $meeting. Any sustained value > 0 (red) means the 16-slot actix mailbox is dropping inbound messages -> video starves -> DecodeBudget caps -> viewport collapse for the whole room. Cross-check with Outbound Queue Depth and Packet Drops/sec. (Populates after backend PR #TBD deploys.)

panels.title: Audio Concealment (expand/sec + %)
panels.description: Unified audio concealment (merges the former rate and percent panels). Concealment rate (videocall_neteq_expand_ops_per_sec, left axis, ops/sec) and concealment % (videocall_audio_concealment_pct, right axis) are the same phenomenon in two units. Sustained nonzero = NetEQ filling audio gaps => audible crackle/dropouts for that pair.

panels.title: Active Transport per Participant (WS vs WT)
panels.description: Each participant's active transport over time, from videocall_client_active_server_rtt_ms broken out by server_type (websocket|webtransport). The RTT value is the line; the server_type in the legend is the transport. A transport flip (WT->WS) or a series gap+reappear is a re-election — correlate with RTT spikes here and Transport Drops/sec. Companion to 'Client RTT to Server'.

panels.title: Transport Re-elections by Outcome (5m)
panels.description: Re-election count + outcome (console-log findings #2/#4, discussion #562). result in {proceeded, aborted, preserved, failed}. 'failed' (red) = 'Election failed: No valid connections' — the on-call signal that a client could not re-establish any transport (often the stale-JWT token-expiry path). videocall_client_reelection_total is a GaugeVec set to the client's CUMULATIVE value, so it is charted with increase() over 5m. Companion to 'Active Transport per Participant'. (Populates after backend PR #TBD deploys.)

panels.title: Adaptive Quality Tier
panels.description: Encoding quality tier (0=best). Rising tiers indicate the adaptive quality system is degrading in response to network/CPU pressure.

panels.title: Reference Loss, Decode Errors & FPS (Receiver)
panels.description: Per-peer receiver health. seq-loss/sec (videocall_video_seq_loss_per_sec, #1013 reference-loss proxy, 570 live series, left axis) is the always-on signal and returns to 0 when healthy — sustained > 0 while FPS still reads ~30 is the classic frozen-but-decoding case. FPS (videocall_video_fps) is plotted on the right axis so it does not flatten the loss series or trip the loss threshold. videocall_decoder_errors_total is a cumulative gauge and videocall_video_frames_dropped latches its last nonzero value (both only update when a hard WebCodecs decode fault occurs) — read these two as 'has a fault occurred', not 'is one occurring right now'. All three loss/error gauges are registered + emitted server-side and conditionally populated.

panels.title: #1013 Reference Loss — Video Seq Loss/sec
panels.description: Per-peer-pair inbound video sequence-number loss rate (lost packets/sec) observed by each receiver — the #1013 reference-loss / freeze signal. A reference frame lost to packet loss freezes the stream until a keyframe arrives, so any SUSTAINED value > 0 (red) for a pair is a freeze red flag, cross-checked against Keyframe Requests/sec (sustained PLI => stream cannot self-recover). The `> 0` filter hides idle/healthy pairs so a busy meeting's chart stays readable. seq-loss is the always-on proxy and returns to 0 when healthy; the exact hard-failure counters (videocall_decoder_errors_total, cumulative; videocall_video_frames_dropped, latches its last nonzero value) are overlaid on the per-receiver panels above. Broken out by reporter → observed peer. Live signal (570 series).

panels.title: Main-Thread Stall vs Render FPS (decode-pipeline vs CPU)
panels.description: Disambiguates the two freeze modes the 06-02 19-person analysis could not separate from Grafana. LONGTASK p95/p99 (videocall_client_longtask_duration_ms, left axis, ms) = main-thread blocking; RENDER FPS (videocall_client_render_fps, right axis) = rAF cadence. High longtask + low render fps + decode-budget NOT capping => main-thread/CPU stall. Decode-budget caps (panels below) WITHOUT high longtask => decode-pipeline/delivery stall. Read alongside the Decode-Budget row directly beneath.

panels.title: Decode Budget: Effective Cap vs Natural
panels.description: Per-session effective visible-tile cap (what the client is decoding) vs the natural/unconstrained tile count. The gap natural - cap = tiles shed to avatars under decode pressure. A widening gap on a session means that client is shedding video.

panels.title: Decode Budget: Sessions Under Pressure
panels.description: Count of sessions with the pressured latch engaged (decode_budget_pressured==1), i.e. actively shedding tiles to avatars. A fleet-wide rise = widespread weak-hardware distress.

panels.title: Decode Budget: Override Mode & Fixed Cap
panels.description: User override mode per session (0=unspecified, 1=auto, 2=fixed) with the chosen fixed cap (override_fixed_n) overlaid. mode=2 means the user manually capped tiles (user-chosen), vs auto-shed by the system - important for triage.

panels.title: Auth Rejections /sec — FLEET-WIDE (not per-meeting)
panels.description: MEETING-AGNOSTIC: videocall_auth_rejections_total carries only a 'reason' label (no meeting_id/room), so this rate is across the WHOLE fleet and is NOT filtered by the $meeting variable. A sustained token_expired rate is the stale-JWT re-election failure (discussion #562) — but you CANNOT attribute it to this meeting from Grafana today (per-meeting attribution is log-only until a room label is added). All reasons are overlaid so you can see the rejection mix.

panels.title: Adaptive Quality Tiers
panels.description: Quality tier per sender (0=best HD, higher=more degraded). Correlate tier changes with target bitrate and encoder queue depth (videocall_encoder_queue_depth, sender-side backpressure) to identify the trigger.

panels.title: Encoder Queue Depth vs Output FPS
panels.description: Sender-side encoder queue depth (a backpressure signal: rising depth means the encoder is falling behind) vs what the encoder actually produces. A growing queue depth indicates the sender is congested enough to justify stepping down quality.

panels.title: Reference Loss & Decode Errors (per receiver)
panels.description: Per-receiver-pair receiver health. seq-loss/sec (videocall_video_seq_loss_per_sec, #1013 reference-loss proxy, 570 live series) is the always-on signal and returns to 0 when healthy. videocall_decoder_errors_total is a cumulative gauge and videocall_video_frames_dropped latches its last nonzero value (both only update on a real WebCodecs decode fault) — read these two as 'has a fault occurred', not 'is one occurring right now'. Correlate with tier changes and encoder queue depth (videocall_encoder_queue_depth, sender-side backpressure) to find whether degradation is isolated or widespread. All three are registered + emitted server-side and conditionally populated.

panels.title: Simulcast / Per-Layer
panels.description: Per-layer simulcast validation (#1083-A3 per-receiver layer divergence) and the #1219 CONGESTION-collapse / #1057 backpressure guards. Relay counters are labeled by 'room' (== meeting name in these tests); publisher gauges are labeled by meeting_id. The fleet-wide outbound-channel-drops panel is NOT filterable by meeting.

panels.title: Per-Layer Forward Mix (relay)
panels.description: Per-layer distribution of forwarded simulcast media packets (layer_id bucketed 0|1|2|other). Stacked = the L0/L1/L2/other mix flowing through the relay. If only layer 0 ever flows, simulcast is inert.

panels.title: % Layer-Filtered (per-receiver selection)
panels.description: Fraction of simulcast packets the relay intentionally dropped because the layer != the receiver's recorded preference. This is the per-receiver layer-divergence signal (#1083-A3): a nonzero fraction means receivers are getting different layer mixes.

panels.title: Publisher Effective vs Active Layers
panels.description: The A3 core-claim panel. effective = ladder depth the publisher is configured to encode; active = layers currently encoding. The effective set should stay constant while one receiver degrades. A gap (active < effective) means AQ shed the top layer(s) at the publisher — a global cut, NOT per-receiver selection.

panels.title: LAYER_PREFERENCE Update Outcomes
panels.description: Outcomes of LAYER_PREFERENCE control updates from receivers (accepted|rate_limited|truncated|layer_id_out_of_bound|ignored_other_subject). Sustained 'rate_limited' or 'layer_id_out_of_bound' means receivers are failing to register their layer preference, which would mask per-receiver divergence.

panels.title: LAYER_HINT Emitted (suppress/restore)
panels.description: LAYER_HINT control packets the relay emits to publishers (direction = suppress|restore). 'suppress' = relay telling a publisher to stop a layer no receiver wants; 'restore' = re-enable it. Activity here is the publish-side feedback loop (#1108 Stage 4).

panels.title: CONGESTION Filtered — non-target (#1220)
panels.description: CONGESTION control packets the relay dropped for non-target receivers (#1220 unicast-correctness coalesce). Validates that a CONGESTION signal is delivered only to the intended receiver, not broadcast — the #1219 collapse fix.

panels.title: #1057 Guard: relay packet drops by reason
panels.description: Relay backpressure loss by drop_reason and transport, filtered to this meeting. A 'mailbox_full' spike (red) is the #1057 room-wide-freeze signature — the 16-slot actix mailbox is dropping inbound messages. This must stay ~0; any sustained nonzero is the freeze precursor.

panels.title: #1057 Guard: outbound channel drops — FLEET-WIDE (not per-meeting)
panels.description: Outbound per-session channel drops by transport and kind. FLEET-WIDE: videocall_outbound_channel_drops_total carries only 'transport' and 'kind' labels (no room/meeting_id), so this is intentionally NOT filtered by $meeting — a $meeting filter would return empty. Read alongside the per-meeting relay-packet-drops panel; this is the cluster-wide backpressure backstop for #1057.
